response.justification: The candidate has genuine geospatial experience (founder of an open-source geo-data platform, Green Map GIS work, FOSS4G speaker) and real low-latency systems work handling millions of requests per second, which maps well to the pipeline and reliability aspects of the role. However, the primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and his only C++ experience dates to 2014–2015; his systems work was in DLang and his recent roles (2020–2025) are TypeScript/Node web development. There is also no direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience, making him an adjacent rather than direct fit.
strengths: Substantial geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (async I/O, caching, fibers) at Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, Linux and Docker fluency
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (brief role in 2014–2015); no Rust
Recent five years focused on TypeScript/web product work, not systems programming
No direct experience with routing engines, navigation guidance, or map-tile production pipelines
